title: Golden Creamy Croatian Pork Pasta
description: A rich and creamy Croatian dish featuring pork, pasta, and a hint of wine, made with strong white bread flour and a mantecare emulsification method, perfect for a weeknight dinner
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pappardelle pasta according to the package instructions until al dente
In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium-high heat and cook the pork tenderloin until browned on all sides
Remove the pork from the skillet and set aside, then add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter to the skillet
Add the strong white bread flour to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly
Slowly add the white wine to the skillet, whisking constantly to avoid lumps
Bring the wine mixture to a simmer and cook until reduced by half, then add the chicken broth and heavy cream to the skillet
Return the pork to the skillet and stir to coat with the cream sauce
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the sauce for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally
Stir in the dried parsley, salt, and black pepper, then serve the pork and sauce over the cooked pappardelle pasta
